account_signals.author: unknown
account_signals.text:
I think ranges/horizontal levels are the most important factor when looking at pairs.

This is because even as value of an asset changes, relative value between two assets should stay the same so long as nothing unique to one of those assets changes. In other words, asset pairs should always trade in a range until something changes that is specific to one or both parts of the pair unequally.

Let's do an example with cars and houses. If an entry level car is $30k, then likely the current entry l

search_signals.text: Trump's pro-crypto stance is generally bullish and can boost sentiment, adoption, and favorable regs—which often correlates with price gains over time. But it doesn't guarantee an immediate or sustained increase. Markets are volatile, driven by macro factors, supply/demand, and global events. Watch volume and news flow for real moves.

This Kraken Fed master account news is a real milestone—first crypto firm with direct access to core US payment rails like Fedwire. It boosts legitimacy and efficiency for fiat-crypto flows.

Broadly bullish for the sector: expect positive sentiment lift for Bitcoin (market leader) and Ethereum (DeFi/staking hub). SOL and others on Kraken could follow too.

Prices are volatile though—many factors at play. Not advice, DYOR.
